title: CUDA kernel for ggml_dsv4_hc_split_sinkhorn (V4 CUDA port Stream B2)

history.note: Plan + spec pre-written and pre-fixed with Stream A lessons (-b CPU,CUDA, count assertion). Stream A merged; B2 unblocked.

Codex r1 REVISE: `-b CPU,CUDA` is invalid — test-backend-ops:9882 parses -b via exact strcmp, so the comma-list silently matches no backend (0/0 pass). Verified codex correct: CUDA device names are CUDA0/CUDA1 (ggml-cuda.cu:4680 + common.cuh:1429 + ggml-cuda.h:17). Fix applied to plan v2 + agent spec: omit -b entirely so harness iterates every non-CPU device and uses CPU as internal reference (test-backend-ops.cpp:9615-9620). This pattern was inherited from the unvalidated pre-fix that all B/C plans share; B2 is the first stream to actually validate it. Codex log: /tmp/codex-v4-cuda-B-sinkhorn-pr.log.

Codex r2 REVISE on v2: gate `| tee | tail` masks test-backend-ops exit status, and `tail -1` reads only the LAST backend summary — on the 2x RTX 6000 Ada dev box, a no-op first device would be silently masked by a passing second device. Fix applied to v3: (a) capture exit status via `RC=$?` after redirect to log, (b) `mapfile` ALL backend summary counts and assert every one >= 4, (c) `grep -q 'Backend.*CUDA'` requires a CUDA backend init line so a degraded CPU-only run can't pass. Plan v3 + agent spec gate updated. Both r1 and r2 surfaced different real defects (not in-circles); v3 closes both. Codex log: /tmp/codex-v4-cuda-B-sinkhorn-pr-r2.log.

Codex r3 REVISE on v3: gate uses `mapfile`, unavailable on macOS Bash 3.2 (orchestrator host). Fix to v4: awk + positional params (set -- $COUNTS) — Bash 3.2 / POSIX-compatible. Plan + agent spec gate updated. Codex log: /tmp/codex-v4-cuda-B-sinkhorn-pr-r3.log. Note: orchestrator host is Darwin arm64 with no nvcc (build-cuda CMakeCache shows CUDAToolkit_NVCC_EXECUTABLE-NOTFOUND); per design spec test-session-scarcity section, B-streams reach merge-ready WITHOUT GPU and the gate runs at the batched validation session. Plan gate is correct for the validation host; on the orchestrator host the gate will hard-fail at cmake-configure, and the task will end in testing state pending validation.

Codex r4 REVISE on v4: `threads_per_block = std::min(256, mix_hc)` produces 24/80 threads for Stream A's cases (n_hc=4 -> mix_dim=24; n_hc=8 -> mix_dim=80). Partial-warp block sizes hit UB in ggml-cuda's block_reduce (common.cuh:599 asserts `block_size % WARP_SIZE == 0`; warp_reduce_sum uses full 0xffffffff mask). Verified codex correct against common.cuh:594-614. Fix to v5: round threads up to next WARP_SIZE multiple, min 32; kernel loops guard `i < mix_hc` so excess threads contribute 0 to SUM reduce. Plan Task 4.1 dispatch + Task 5.1 kernel pseudocode updated. Codex log: /tmp/codex-v4-cuda-B-sinkhorn-pr-r4.log.

Codex r5 REVISE on v5: ALGORITHMIC ERROR — Task 5.1 pseudocode said 'normalize entire mix_hc vector' which would silently produce garbage. CPU reference (ops.cpp:11037-11117) splits output into THREE sections: (1) pre slice out[0:n_hc] = sigmoid(mix[i]*pre_scale+base[i])+eps; (2) post slice out[n_hc:2*n_hc] = 2*sigmoid(mix[off]*post_scale+base[off]); (3) ONLY comb matrix out[2*n_hc:mix_hc] (n_hc*n_hc floats) gets the actual Sinkhorn algorithm — first iter is per-dst_hc softmax (max-subtract + exp + normalize + eps), then alternating column/row normalizations for sinkhorn_iters-1 more iterations. Verified codex correct by reading CPU reference directly. Fix to v6: full rewrite of Task 5.1 pseudocode as line-by-line translation of CPU reference; Section 3 done serially on tid==0 since n_hc<=16 (asserted at ops.cpp:11014). Step 5.2 (blockReduceSum helper) deleted as moot. Task 4.1 dispatch assertions tightened (n_hc, sinkhorn_iters, mix_hc == (2+n_hc)*n_hc, nb[0] == sizeof(float)). Codex log: /tmp/codex-v4-cuda-B-sinkhorn-pr-r5.log. This is the 5th distinct real defect and the most consequential — would have produced silent NMSE failures at gate. Pre-fix from Stream A only addressed harness-correctness, not algorithm-correctness; deep review caught all 5.

Codex r6 APPROVE on v6. Cross-verified v6 Task 5.1 against ops.cpp:11037-11117 directly: 3-section split, comb indexing src_hc + dst_hc*n_hc, initial per-dst_hc softmax with +eps, first column-normalize, remaining row/col-normalize loop, final copy all match CPU implementation. Dispatch constraints aligned with CPU assertions, block sizing handles 24/80/288-float cases, gate avoids 0/0, masked-exit, Bash 3.2 issues. CUDA validation pending by environment design (no nvcc on orchestrator host). Total: 5 REVISE rounds (each a distinct real defect), 1 APPROVE. Transitioning to implementing with worktree-isolated builder. Codex log: /tmp/codex-v4-cuda-B-sinkhorn-pr-r6.log.
